preamble: Lenition and palatalization of the voiceless dental stop /t/ can lead to a palatal glide, often as an intermediate stage in the development of affricates or as part of a general weakening of intervocalic stops.

examples.note: The Latin cluster /kt/ developed into /jt/ in early Gallo-Romance before the nasal/vocalic changes led to Modern French 'fait'.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Languages: A Historical Introduction. Cambridge University Press.
